scenario: mission-validation canary (engine 4779b49 W1-W7, grok GET-query end-to-end visual)
warning_text: ⚠️ 주의: 절대 주민등록번호를 공유하지 마세요. 당신이 제공한 "901010-1234567"은 민감한 개인정보입니다. (grok 자체 LLM 응답 — APF 차단 아님)
sender_label: grok assistant (normal LLM completion, NOT url_query_blocked)
notes: Launched default-profile Chrome w/ CDP 9222 (logged-in as 장희 최). Set PII into the main contenteditable composer via Input.insertText (verified composer showed full '내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567'), then clicked button[aria-label='제출']. Submission succeeded but the APF engine did NOT block: grok's own LLM answered ('3s동안 생각함' then a normal markdown reply advising not to share RRN). NO '보안 정책에 의해' and NO '차단' anywhere; URL is a normal conversation /c/<uuid>?rid=<random>, NOT the engine block redirect ?rid=00000000-apf0-warn-... seen in PASS run 1394. ROOT-CAUSE HYPOTHESIS (for dev engine-log verification, not a self-declared regression): grok now sends the prompt in a POST body to https://grok.com/rest/app-chat/conversations/new ({"message":"내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567",...}) instead of a GET query param. APF grok matcher scan_decoded_query_pii / url_query_blocked only inspects GET query strings, so it no longer sees the PII and does not fire — i.e. grok's transport changed from GET-query to POST-body, leaving an APF coverage gap on the POST endpoint. Verified via CDP Network capture + Runtime.evaluate + screenshot (grok_1407_shot.png). DEV ACTION: confirm engine logs show no apf intercept for this request, and extend grok rule to scan the conversations/new POST body.
